name: The Constitution of the United States
desc: The supreme law of the United States, ratified in 1788. Establishes three branches of government with separation of powers and a system of checks and balances. All federal government authority flows from this document.

children.name: Legislative Branch — The Congress
children.desc: Article I branch vested with all legislative powers. Consists of the Senate and House of Representatives. Responsible for making federal law, declaring war, controlling federal spending, and conducting oversight of the executive branch.

children.name: United States Senate
children.desc: Upper chamber of Congress. 100 senators (2 per state), 6-year staggered terms. Confirms presidential nominees, ratifies treaties, tries impeachments.

children.name: Senate Leadership
children.desc: Elected and appointed leadership positions directing the Senate's business.

children.name: President of the Senate (Vice President)
children.desc: Constitutional role; presides over Senate, casts tie-breaking votes.

children.name: President Pro Tempore
children.desc: Presides in VP's absence; traditionally the most senior majority senator.

children.name: Majority Leader
children.desc: Leads the majority party; schedules all Senate floor business.

children.name: Minority Leader
children.desc: Leads the minority party; negotiates with Majority Leader.

children.name: Majority Whip
children.desc: Ensures majority party attendance and vote discipline.

children.name: Minority Whip
children.desc: Ensures minority party attendance and vote discipline.

children.name: Assistant Majority Leader
children.desc: Assists Majority Whip with floor operations.

children.name: Assistant Minority Leader
children.desc: Assists Minority Whip with floor operations.

children.name: Majority Conference Chair
children.desc: Chairs majority party conference meetings.

children.name: Minority Conference Chair
children.desc: Chairs minority party conference meetings.

children.name: Majority Conference Secretary
children.desc: Records and distributes majority conference proceedings.

children.name: Majority Policy Committee Chair
children.desc: Develops and communicates majority policy positions.

children.name: Minority Policy Committee Chair
children.desc: Develops and communicates minority policy positions.

children.name: Majority Steering Committee Chair
children.desc: Oversees majority party committee assignments.

children.name: Majority Campaign Committee Chair
children.desc: Leads majority party Senate campaign organization (DSCC or NRSC).

children.name: Senate Committees
children.desc: 20 standing committees and several select committees that conduct the legislative work of the Senate.

children.name: Individual Senator Offices (100)
children.desc: Each of 100 senators maintains a Washington D.C. office and state offices. Structure shown is representative.

children.name: Chief of Staff
children.desc: Manages overall office operations and strategic direction.

children.name: Deputy Chief of Staff
children.desc: Assists the Chief of Staff; oversees day-to-day operations.

children.name: Legislative Director
children.desc: Manages all legislative activities and staff portfolio.

children.name: Legislative Counsel
children.desc: Provides legal analysis on legislation and amendments.

children.name: Senior Legislative Assistant (×3)
children.desc: Develops and tracks legislation in assigned policy areas.

children.name: Legislative Correspondent
children.desc: Drafts responses to constituent correspondence on policy matters.

children.name: Communications Director
children.desc: Manages all media relations and public messaging.

children.name: Deputy Communications Director
children.desc: Assists with press operations and social media.

children.name: Press Secretary
children.desc: Handles day-to-day media inquiries and press releases.

children.name: Digital Director
children.desc: Manages social media channels and digital communications strategy.

children.name: State Director
children.desc: Oversees all in-state constituent services and offices.

children.name: Deputy State Director
children.desc: Assists the State Director with constituent services.

children.name: Regional Representative (×4)
children.desc: Serves as liaison between Senator and constituents in assigned region.

children.name: Scheduler / Executive Assistant
children.desc: Manages the Senator's calendar and travel arrangements.

children.name: Casework Director
children.desc: Oversees constituent casework operations with federal agencies.

children.name: Senior Caseworker (×4)
children.desc: Assists constituents with federal agency issues (SSA, VA, immigration, etc.).

children.name: Office Manager
children.desc: Manages office administration, supplies, and facilities.

children.name: Staff Assistant
children.desc: Handles phones, mail, tours, and flag requests.

children.name: Senate Administrative Offices
children.desc: Non-partisan offices that support the administrative, financial, and operational functions of the Senate.

children.name: Secretary of the Senate
children.desc: Chief administrative and financial officer of the Senate. Maintains official records and manages 17 sub-offices.

children.name: Secretary of the Senate
children.desc: Constitutional officer; chief administrative officer of the Senate.

children.name: Deputy Secretary of the Senate
children.desc: Assists the Secretary with all administrative functions.

children.name: Assistant Secretary of the Senate
children.desc: Manages specific administrative functions as assigned.

children.name: Bill Clerk
children.desc: Receives, numbers, and tracks all Senate bills and resolutions.

children.name: Journal Clerk
children.desc: Maintains the official Journal of the Senate.

children.name: Executive Clerk
children.desc: Maintains executive business records; processes nominations and treaties.

children.name: Enrolling Clerk
children.desc: Enrolls passed legislation for presidential signature.

children.name: Legislative Information Officer
children.desc: Manages Senate's online legislative information systems.

children.name: Senate Librarian
children.desc: Manages the Senate Library collection and reference services.

children.name: Senate Curator
children.desc: Preserves Senate art, artifacts, and historical collections.

children.name: Senate Historian
children.desc: Documents Senate history and manages the historical archives.

children.name: Director of Senate Security
children.desc: Oversees security of classified information for the Senate.

children.name: Director of Public Records
children.desc: Manages public disclosure of financial and lobbying records.

children.name: Director of Human Resources
children.desc: Administers Senate HR policies and employee benefits.

children.name: Director of Finance
children.desc: Manages Senate payroll and financial disbursements.

children.name: Director of the Page Program
children.desc: Manages the Senate Page program (when active).

children.name: Director of the Capitol Printing & Folding Room
children.desc: Manages official Senate printing and mail distribution.

children.name: Sergeant at Arms of the Senate
children.desc: Enforces Senate rules, manages Capitol Police liaison, and oversees all Senate operational services.

children.name: Senate Chaplain

children.name: United States House of Representatives
children.desc: Lower chamber of Congress. 435 voting members apportioned by state population. 2-year terms. Originates all revenue bills; initiates impeachment proceedings.

children.name: House Leadership
children.desc: Elected leadership positions that manage House floor operations and party strategy.

children.name: Speaker of the House
children.desc: Presides over the House; 2nd in line of presidential succession; sets legislative agenda.

children.name: Speaker Pro Tempore
children.desc: Presides in Speaker's absence.

children.name: Majority Leader
children.desc: Floor leader for the majority party; schedules legislation.

children.name: Minority Leader
children.desc: Floor leader for the minority party.

children.name: Majority Whip
children.desc: Counts and secures majority party votes.

children.name: Minority Whip
children.desc: Counts and secures minority party votes.

children.name: Chief Deputy Majority Whip
children.desc: Assists Majority Whip with vote counting and member relations.

children.name: Chief Deputy Minority Whip
children.desc: Assists Minority Whip.

children.name: Democratic Caucus Chair
children.desc: Chairs House Democratic Caucus meetings.

children.name: Democratic Caucus Vice Chair
children.desc: Assists Democratic Caucus Chair.

children.name: Republican Conference Chair
children.desc: Chairs House Republican Conference meetings.

children.name: Republican Conference Vice Chair
children.desc: Assists Republican Conference Chair.

children.name: Republican Conference Secretary
children.desc: Records Republican Conference proceedings.

children.name: Democratic Steering & Policy Committee Chair
children.desc: Oversees Democratic committee assignments and policy.

children.name: Individual Representative Offices (435)
children.desc: Each of 435 representatives maintains a Washington office and district offices. Structure shown is representative.

children.name: Chief of Staff
children.desc: Manages all office operations and serves as top advisor.

children.name: Deputy Chief of Staff
children.desc: Assists Chief of Staff; often manages D.C. or district operations.

children.name: Legislative Director
children.desc: Directs legislative agenda and manages legislative team.

children.name: Senior Legislative Assistant (×2)
children.desc: Handles major policy areas and committee work.

children.name: Legislative Assistant (×3)
children.desc: Tracks legislation and drafts policy memos in assigned areas.

children.name: Legislative Correspondent
children.desc: Responds to constituent mail on legislative issues.

children.name: Staff Assistant
children.desc: Entry-level; answers phones, manages tours, processes mail.

children.name: Communications Director
children.desc: Manages all press and communications strategy.

children.name: Press Secretary
children.desc: Handles media inquiries and social media.

children.name: District Director
children.desc: Manages all district operations and constituent services.

children.name: Deputy District Director
children.desc: Assists District Director; may oversee specific counties.

children.name: Constituent Services Director
children.desc: Oversees casework team helping constituents with federal agencies.

children.name: Caseworker (×3)
children.desc: Assists constituents with SSA, VA, immigration, and other agency issues.

children.name: Field Representative (×2)
children.desc: Represents the Member at community events in the district.

children.name: Scheduler
children.desc: Manages the Member's calendar and arranges travel.

children.name: House Administrative Offices
children.desc: Non-partisan offices providing administrative, legal, and operational support to the House.

children.name: Clerk of the House
children.desc: Official record keeper; certifies legislation; manages election-related records.

children.name: Clerk of the House
children.desc: Federal position: Clerk of the House

children.name: Deputy Clerk
children.desc: Federal position: Deputy Clerk

children.desc: Federal position: Director of Legislative Resource Center

children.desc: Federal position: Director of House History & Preservation

children.desc: Federal position: Director of Communications

children.name: Director of Human Resources

children.name: Director of Information Systems
children.desc: Federal position: Director of Information Systems

children.name: Sergeant at Arms of the House
children.desc: Enforces House rules, maintains order, manages Capitol access and House security operations.

children.name: Chief Administrative Officer
children.desc: Manages House financial, HR, IT, and administrative services for all Member offices.

children.name: House Chaplain
children.desc: Opens each session with prayer; provides pastoral services to Members and staff.

children.name: Office of the Parliamentarian
children.desc: Advises the Speaker and all Members on House rules and procedures.

children.name: Office of Legislative Counsel
children.desc: Drafts and reviews legislation in non-partisan service to all Members.

children.name: Office of the Attending Physician
children.desc: Provides emergency and primary medical care to Members, officers, and staff.

children.name: Office of the Inspector General
children.desc: Conducts audits and investigations of House operations.

children.name: Executive Branch
children.desc: Article II branch headed by the President. Responsible for executing and enforcing federal law. Encompasses the Executive Office of the President, 15 cabinet departments, and 200+ independent agencies.

children.name: The President of the United States
children.desc: Commander-in-Chief. Signs or vetoes legislation. Appoints federal judges, ambassadors, and cabinet secretaries. 4-year term, maximum 2 terms.

children.name: The Vice President of the United States
children.desc: President of the Senate (casts tie-breaking votes). First in line of presidential succession. Member of NSC and National Security Council.

children.name: Executive Office of the President (EOP)
children.desc: The central policy and management apparatus of the U.S. government. Comprises ~1,800 staff serving the President across multiple offices and councils.

children.name: White House Office

children.name: The Cabinet — Executive Departments
children.desc: 15 executive departments led by secretaries (or the Attorney General for DOJ). All confirmed by the Senate. Collectively advise the President and execute federal law.

children.name: Department of State
children.desc: Conducts U.S. foreign policy, manages diplomatic relations with 190+ countries, issues passports and visas, and protects Americans abroad.

children.name: Secretary of Department of State
children.desc: Principal head of the department; cabinet-level officer appointed by the President.

children.name: Deputy Secretary of Department of State
children.desc: Second-in-command; acts as Secretary when absent.

children.name: Chief of Staff
children.desc: Manages day-to-day operations of the Secretary's office.

children.name: Executive Secretary
children.desc: Coordinates executive correspondence and department-wide communications.

children.name: Inspector General
children.desc: Conducts audits and investigations to prevent fraud, waste, and abuse.

children.name: Deputy Inspector General
children.desc: Assists IG; oversees audit or investigation divisions.

children.name: General Counsel
children.desc: Chief legal officer; advises on all legal matters.

children.name: Deputy General Counsel
children.desc: Assists General Counsel across all legal divisions.

children.name: Chief Financial Officer
children.desc: Oversees budget, accounting, and financial management.

children.name: Deputy CFO / Controller
children.desc: Manages financial operations, grants management, and reporting.

children.name: Chief Information Officer
children.desc: Oversees IT systems, cybersecurity, and digital services.

children.name: Deputy CIO
children.desc: Assists CIO; often manages specific IT programs or cybersecurity.

children.name: Chief Human Capital Officer
children.desc: Directs workforce management, hiring, and employee relations.

children.name: Director of Legislative Affairs
children.desc: Manages the department's relationships with Congress.

children.name: Director of Public Affairs
children.desc: Oversees external communications and press operations.

children.name: Diversity & Inclusion Officer
children.desc: Advances equity and inclusion programs across the department.

children.name: Department of Homeland Security (DHS)
children.desc: Created in 2002 after 9/11. Coordinates national efforts against terrorism, natural disasters, and border security. Third-largest cabinet department.

children.name: Secretary of Department of Homeland Security (DHS)
children.desc: Principal head of the department; cabinet-level officer appointed by the President.

children.name: Deputy Secretary of Department of Homeland Security (DHS)
children.desc: Second-in-command; acts as Secretary when absent.

children.name: Chief of Staff
children.desc: Manages day-to-day operations of the Secretary's office.

children.name: Executive Secretary
children.desc: Coordinates executive correspondence and department-wide communications.

children.name: Inspector General
children.desc: Conducts audits and investigations to prevent fraud, waste, and abuse.

children.name: Deputy Inspector General
children.desc: Assists IG; oversees audit or investigation divisions.

children.name: General Counsel
children.desc: Chief legal officer; advises on all legal matters.

children.name: Deputy General Counsel
children.desc: Assists General Counsel across all legal divisions.

children.name: Chief Financial Officer
children.desc: Oversees budget, accounting, and financial management.

children.name: Deputy CFO / Controller
children.desc: Manages financial operations, grants management, and reporting.

children.name: Chief Information Officer
children.desc: Oversees IT systems, cybersecurity, and digital services.

children.name: Deputy CIO
children.desc: Assists CIO; often manages specific IT programs or cybersecurity.

children.name: Chief Human Capital Officer
children.desc: Directs workforce management, hiring, and employee relations.

children.name: Director of Legislative Affairs
children.desc: Manages the department's relationships with Congress.

children.name: Director of Public Affairs
children.desc: Oversees external communications and press operations.

children.name: Diversity & Inclusion Officer
children.desc: Advances equity and inclusion programs across the department.

children.desc: Presides over oral arguments and conference. Assigns majority opinions when in majority. Also presides over Senate impeachment trials.

children.name: Associate Justice (×8)
children.desc: Hear cases, write opinions, vote on petitions. Each maintains chambers with 4 law clerks.

children.name: Clerk of the Court
children.desc: Manages Supreme Court administrative operations; processes all filings.

children.name: Marshal of the Supreme Court
children.desc: Security and facilities management; maintains order in Court.

children.name: Reporter of Decisions
children.desc: Edits and publishes the official United States Reports.

children.name: All 94 District Courts — Standard Structure
children.desc: Each district has a Chief Judge, district judges, magistrate judges, bankruptcy judges, and supporting staff.

children.name: Chief Judge
children.desc: Senior active judge; administrative head of the district.

children.name: District Judge (×varies per district)
children.desc: Article III judge with lifetime appointment.

children.name: Magistrate Judge (×varies)
children.desc: Handles pretrial matters, misdemeanors, and by consent civil cases.

children.name: Bankruptcy Judge (×varies)
children.desc: Handles bankruptcy cases (Chapters 7, 11, 12, 13).

children.name: Clerk of Court
children.desc: Administrative head of the clerk's office.

children.desc: Manages probation and supervised release.

children.desc: Manages each judge's docket and courtroom operations.

children.desc: 1-2 year clerkship; assists judge with legal research.

children.desc: Contract security screening access to courthouse.

children.name: Court Reporter / ECRO
children.desc: Records official verbatim record of proceedings.
